title: Tarrietia parvifolia
taxonomy: Tarrietia parvifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, commonly known as the soapberry family. This family includes over 140 genera and more than 1,500 species of trees and shrubs. Tarrietia parvifolia is one of the lesser-known species within this family. The classification of Tarrietia parvifolia is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification includes the order Sapindales, the family Sapindaceae, and the genus Tarrietia.
uses: Tarrietia parvifolia, a plant native to Australia, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The leaves of this plant are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. It is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against bacterial and fungal infections. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. More research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses of Tarrietia parvifolia, but its potential benefits are promising.

It has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
